TimeEntry.Title: Re: Brandywine Remote Access
TimeEntry.Notes:
The user, Jason, reported an issue with remote access. Provided the following instructions to start a remote session using MyRemote2:

  1. Open https://myremote2.technijian.com.

  2. Click on “Start a new session”.

  3. Enter the name “Jason”.

  4. Download and launch the setup file (double-click to run).

    • In case of multiple access confirmations, instruct the user to click Yes, Run, or Allow.

Informed Jason to confirm once he successfully joins the session.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Brandywine Remote Access
TimeEntry.Notes: User is unable to login to VPN- tunnelbrick. User has mac, it asked for security code. user profile is set to connect automatically. we are unable to see the window to enter creditials. i suggest first to remove automatic then try.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Brandywine Remote Access
TimeEntry.Notes: Intially was trying to connect to vpn using sophos, but sophos central was not opening. We notced user password was not working on sophos login. chnaged the password, it didnt work. Next deleted the profile from the sophos firewall. Next we asked user jason to get MFA on phone. Sophos need tunnel blink to work on mac. Installed tunnel blink, uploaded the configuration file. did reamaing set up. VPN got connected to BWH server. User wanted to know how to access newstar live. showed a demonstration to the user.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: Brandywine Remote Access
TimeEntry.Notes: Created instruction (step by step) and sent it to Jason.

TimeEntry.Title: Brandywine Connection not working again
TimeEntry.Notes:
Jason reported an issue regarding the VPN connectivity issue....

so connected to the sysem and Disconnect the previour session and reconnect it again...

VPN is connectd and now check the remote apps.....

User is able to access the remote apps as well....

no more issue reported by the user......hence closing the  ticket......

TimeEntry.Title: CBI– Network Detective Report Generated and Updated Under Teams
TimeEntry.Notes: As per assigned task by lead, Network Detective reports were generated successfully for CBI Client. Checked and verified the reports are proper. Then uploaded the report to the appropriate client Teams channel for CBI. It is now ready for review and take actions on alerts.

TimeEntry.Title: Huntress Agent updated with Version V0.14.132 on CBILT06,CBILT09,CBILT10
TimeEntry.Notes:
Login into the Huntress portal, we verified that the Huntress Agent Version on the following machines on  is old version V0.14.96. Connected to the machines
CBILT06,CBILT09,CBILT10 from manage engine, download and installed the latest version Ver0.14.132. After that verified in the portal it is updated to latest version.

TimeEntry.Title: Manage-Engine Latest version updated 11.5.2600.19.W - CBILT12,CBILT09,CBILT06
TimeEntry.Notes:
Review the My RMM portal, and found the machine  CBILT12,CBILT09,CBILT06 running with old version of 
Manage-Engine. So connected to the machine  and then uninstalled the OLD version and downloaded and installed the latest
version (11.5.2600.19.W) of the manage-engine agent. Verified from the portal, device is running with updated agent version.

TimeEntry.Title: CBILT06,CBILT12 : Found cisco umbrella old version, update with latest (5.1.14.145)
TimeEntry.Notes: I accessed both machines, downloaded the latest package (Cisco Secure Client 5.1.14.145), and completed the update successfully.
TimeEntry.Resource: S. Kumar Sharma CHD-TS1 Support - Remote

TimeEntry.Title: Dynamic Group Creation & Huntress Learner Sync Configuration for CBI
TimeEntry.Notes: Configured a new Microsoft Entra ID Dynamic User Group specifically designed to include only licensed mailbox-enabled users. Created the group using a validated dynamic membership rule that filters for active, member-type accounts with an enabled Exchange Online service plan, ensuring only real, licensed users are included. After successfully creating and verifying the dynamic group membership logic, proceeded to integrate Huntress SAT with Microsoft Graph. In the Huntress portal, navigated to Integrations → Providers, selected the CBI tenant, and chose the newly created dynamic user group (CBI_Users) as the group to sync learners from. Enabled the option to exclude unlicensed and non-human learner identities, ensuring only properly licensed human users can be provisioned as Huntress learners. Confirmed that Use UPN as unique email address was selected to maintain identity accuracy. Activated Automatic Daily Sync so learner accounts are continuously updated based on group membership changes. Also enabled attribute sharing options (Status, First Name, Last Name, Employee ID, Department, Manager) and verified advanced settings related to learner activation/inactivation and department creation. Completed validation of the sync configuration to ensure that only authorized, licensed users will populate within Huntress SAT moving forward
